title: silene vulgaris subsp. vourinensis
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. vourinensis, commonly known as the Balkan catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming the plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antitumor properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
